Name: UN Headquarters
Why it is good: Home of UN Open Source Week — the anchor for all OSW+ events
Dietary notes: N/A
Tips: Visitor access requires pre-registration; nearby Visitor Entrance on 45th St

Name: Gregory's Coffee (3rd Ave)
Why it is good: NYC-born specialty coffee chain with fast service and reliable espresso; good WiFi and enough seating for a small sidebar
Dietary notes: Vegan milk options; pastries include GF choices
Tips: Busy 8–9 AM rush — arrive early or after 9:30; power outlets along the window counter

Name: Joe Coffee
Why it is good: NYC independent specialty roaster known for quality single-origin pours and a calm atmosphere — ideal for 1:1 chats
Dietary notes: Several dairy-free milk options; pastries vary
Tips: Less crowded than chains; good for laptop work; located near Grand Central

Name: Birch Coffee
Why it is good: Community-focused NYC coffee shop with cozy seating and board games — a favorite among locals for longer conversations
Dietary notes: Oat and almond milk; gluten-free snacks available
Tips: Great for relaxed mid-afternoon meetups; quieter than street-level cafes

Name: Dag Hammarskjöld Plaza
Address: E 47th St between 1st Ave & 2nd Ave, New York, NY 10017
Why it is good: Named after the second UN Secretary-General — a beautifully maintained plaza with benches and seasonal farmers market; perfect spontaneous meeting spot right next to the UN
Dietary notes: Outdoor only
Tips: Farmers market Wednesdays year-round; great backdrop for photos; mostly locals

Name: Tudor City Greens
Address: Tudor City Place, New York, NY 10017
Why it is good: Two elevated private parks (publicly accessible) with benches and leafy calm — hidden gem just above 42nd St with a view of the UN and Chrysler Building
Dietary notes: Outdoor only
Tips: Enter via stairs on 42nd St between 1st and 2nd Ave; peaceful escape from Midtown bustle

Name: East River Esplanade
Address: East River Greenway, New York, NY 10017
Why it is good: Paved waterfront path along the East River with benches and views of Queens and the bridges — great for a walking meeting or short break
Dietary notes: Outdoor path
Tips: Access from the bottom of 42nd or 47th St; path runs north to the 50s easily

Name: Greenacre Park
Why it is good: Pocket park with a dramatic 25-ft waterfall that masks city noise — one of NYC's best vest-pocket parks for a quiet conversation or recharge
Dietary notes: Seasonal snack bar on-site
Tips: Open daily; free to enter; the waterfall sound makes it perfect for private conversations even in the city

Name: Xi'an Famous Foods
Why it is good: Hand-ripped noodles and lamb burgers from the Xi'an region of China — bold flavors at very affordable prices; beloved NYC chain that is always worth the short wait
Dietary notes: Vegetarian and vegan options available; spice level adjustable
Tips: Quick counter service; cash or card; seating is limited so best for takeout to a nearby plaza

Name: Hale and Hearty
Why it is good: NYC-born soup and salad chain known for creative rotating soups and big grain bowls — fast and filling for a conference lunch
Dietary notes: Vegetarian
Tips: vegan

Name: Ammos Estiatorio
Why it is good: Upscale Greek seafood restaurant with a great-value lunch prix fixe — impressive enough for an important side meeting without blowing the budget
Dietary notes: Seafood-forward; good vegetarian mezze options; gluten-free available
Tips: Lunch prix fixe (weekdays) is excellent value; dinner more expensive; reservations recommended for groups

Name: Halal Guys (53rd & 6th Ave)
Address: W 53rd St & 6th Ave, New York, NY 10019
Why it is good: The original NYC halal cart that launched a global chain — chicken and rice with white sauce and hot sauce at unbeatable prices
Dietary notes: Halal; no pork; vegetarian falafel option
Tips: This is the original outdoor cart (open until late); the brick-and-mortar franchise is nearby too; cash preferred at the cart

Name: 1st Ave Halal Cart
Address: 1st Ave & E 46th St, New York, NY 10017
Why it is good: Classic NYC street-cart halal food — chicken over rice with salad and sauces for under $10; as close to the UN as it gets
Dietary notes: Halal; vegetarian rice and salad combo available
Tips: Cart positions shift; look along 1st Ave between 44th–47th St; cash only; perfect for a quick affordable lunch

Name: Benjamin Steakhouse
Why it is good: Elegant USDA prime dry-aged steakhouse in a stunning Art Deco Chemists Club building — ideal for a celebratory dinner or high-stakes evening meeting
Dietary notes: Non-beef options available; accommodates dietary restrictions with advance notice
Tips: Reservations strongly recommended; business casual dress; prix fixe lunch is the best value

Name: The Water Club
Address: East River at 30th St, New York, NY 10016
Why it is good: Unique NYC landmark — a restaurant on a permanently moored barge on the East River with sweeping Manhattan and river views; memorable dinner setting for an international delegation
Dietary notes: Seafood-focused; vegetarian and dietary accommodations available
Tips: Reservations essential; take a taxi or Uber rather than walking at night; Sunday brunch is iconic

Name: The Ginger Man
Why it is good: Beer bar with 70+ taps featuring craft and import ales — relaxed atmosphere perfect for post-conference pints with a global crowd
Dietary notes: Pub food with vegetarian options; large gluten-free beer selection
Tips: Happy hour Mon–Fri 3–6 PM; large comfortable booths good for groups; quieter than midtown tourist bars

Name: Turtle Bay Brasserie
Why it is good: Neighborhood French-American brasserie and bar in the heart of Turtle Bay — great happy hour and sidewalk seating; a local favorite for UN and NGO staff
Dietary notes: Vegetarian and vegan menu items; gluten-free options
Tips: Happy hour weekdays 4–7 PM; can get loud later in the evening; good spot for spontaneous after-work gatherings

Name: Wheeltapper Pub
Address: 89 E 42nd St (inside Grand Central), New York, NY 10017
Why it is good: Classic pub inside Grand Central Terminal serving craft beers and hearty food — legendary location in one of the world's greatest train stations
Dietary notes: Standard pub menu with vegetarian options
Tips: Convenient for anyone arriving by train; gets busy at rush hour; beautiful interior inside Grand Central

Name: Osteria Laguna
Address: 209 E 42nd St, New York, NY 10017, United States

Name: DRINK33
Why it is good: Compact neighborhood bar with a strong rating and a late-night crowd; good for casual drinks close to Midtown South.
Dietary notes: Bar menu; check for snacks and late bites
Tips: Best for a small group; opens in the evening.

Name: Playwright Irish Pub
Why it is good: Irish pub with a central Midtown location and strong reviews; easy meetup spot near Herald Square.
Dietary notes: Pub food; check menu for vegetarian options
Tips: Good for a pre- or post-event pint near Penn Station and Herald Square.

Name: Clinton Hall
Why it is good: Busy beer bar and gastropub with a group-friendly layout and solid reviews.
Dietary notes: Pub fare; vegetarian options
Tips: Can get lively; best for groups.

Name: Sundays Well
Why it is good: Neighborhood gastropub with a relaxed Murray Hill vibe and solid ratings.
Dietary notes: Pub fare; vegetarian options
Tips: Good for after-work drinks; check peak hours.

Name: The Junction
Why it is good: Lexington Avenue bar with pub fare and a neighborhood feel; convenient for a quick post-event pint.
Dietary notes: Pub fare; vegetarian options
Tips: Easy casual meetup; pub crowd.

Name: Talea Beer Co. Bryant Park
Why it is good: Bright beer hall near Bryant Park with a strong local following and plenty of room for small groups.
Dietary notes: Beer and bar snacks; check menu for dietary needs
Tips: Good for a drink before or after Bryant Park events; book if you want a table.

Name: Parnell's
Why it is good: Classic Irish pub with a big 1st Avenue footprint and reliable late hours.
Dietary notes: Pub fare; vegetarian options
Tips: Late hours; good fallback when nearby bars are full.

Name: Agency of Record
Why it is good: Cocktail bar in the Renwick Hotel with polished drinks and a more elevated vibe than a standard pub.
Dietary notes: Cocktail menu; light bites
Tips: Reserve if you want a table; hotel bar setting.

Name: Albert's Bar
Why it is good: Cocktail bar near the UN with strong reviews and a mid-range price point.
Dietary notes: Cocktail menu; check menu
Tips: Best for a smaller group or quieter drink.

Name: Vintage Green Rooftop
Why it is good: All-season rooftop lounge near Grand Central with skyline views and event-friendly cocktail service.
Dietary notes: Cocktails and small plates
Tips: Book ahead; rooftop views make it a splurge option.
